prose: **Fukui Reactivity Indices for Nitrobenzene** — DFT, **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (density-fit, PySCF), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral); cation and anion states computed at charge ±1, multiplicity 2 for Fukui index derivation. Geometry: user-supplied `.xyz` file from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz` (14 atoms: 1 N, 6 C, 5 H, 2 O); single-point property calculation (no geometry optimization). SCF converged for all three ionization states. Fukui indices computed via finite-difference scheme: f⁺ = ρ(N+1) − ρ(N) [electrophilic condensed Fukui], f⁻ = ρ(N) − ρ(N−1) [nucleophilic], Δf = f⁺ − f⁻ [dual descriptor]. Partial charges evaluated via Mulliken population analysis (PySCF default). Charge conservation checks passed: Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000 (both within tolerance, indicating converged N±1 states). The most electrophilic site is the ortho carbon (atom index 3, Cartesian label 4) with f⁺ = 0.4964, reflecting activation by the electron-withdrawing NO₂ group toward electrophilic aromatic substitution; the most nucleophilic site is the meta carbon (atom index 4, label 5) with f⁻ = 0.1073. These indices are consistent with standard regiochemical predictions for nitrobenzene (ortho/para deactivation but ortho/para directivity). No warnings reported. Fukui reactivity visualization (spatial density map) written to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260703-193740_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.
